text: FastAPI supports fully async database connections via SQLAlchemy 2.0 with asyncpg driver. Use create_async_engine and AsyncSession to handle concurrent requests without blocking the event loop.

Flask is a microframework focused on simplicity. It uses WSGI and is synchronous by default. For async support you need Flask 2.0+ with the async/await keywords, but it does not scale as well as ASGI frameworks.
Django is a batteries-included framework with a powerful ORM, admin panel, and authentication. It supports async views since 3.1 but the ORM itself became async-compatible only in Django 4.1.
When using FastAPI with async database connections, you must avoid blocking calls. Use asyncpg or aiomysql and always await queries inside async def endpoints to prevent event loop stalls.
Gunicorn is a pre-fork WSGI server for production Python deployments. It is commonly used with Flask and Django, paired with nginx as a reverse proxy for serving static files and handling TLS.
Uvicorn is an ASGI server designed for async frameworks like FastAPI and Starlette. For production you typically run gunicorn with uvicorn workers: gunicorn -k uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker main:app.
Deploying a Python web app to production usually involves gunicorn or uvicorn behind nginx, systemd for process management, and a reverse proxy for SSL. Docker is the modern standard for packaging.

REST APIs follow HTTP semantics with resource-based URLs and standard verbs like GET, POST, PUT, DELETE. They are simple to cache and debug but can lead to over-fetching when clients need partial data.
GraphQL lets clients request exactly the fields they need in a single query. It shines for complex nested data but adds server-side complexity and makes caching harder than REST.

FastAPI is one of the fastest Python web frameworks thanks to its async-first design, type hints, and automatic OpenAPI schema generation. Benchmarks show it 2-3x faster than Flask for I/O bound workloads.
Pytest is the de-facto testing framework for Python. Use pytest fixtures for setup, pytest-asyncio for testing async code, and pytest-cov for coverage reports.
SQLAlchemy 2.0 introduces a unified sync/async API. The new style uses select() statements and session.execute() instead of the legacy Query API.
Django Rest Framework (DRF) is the most popular way to build REST APIs in Django. It provides serializers, viewsets, authentication, and automatic browsable API documentation.

Alembic handles database migrations for SQLAlchemy. Generate migrations with alembic revision --autogenerate and apply with alembic upgrade head. Always review auto-generated migrations before applying.
Celery is the standard task queue for Python web apps. Use it with Redis or RabbitMQ as the broker for async background jobs like sending emails or processing uploads.
Pydantic is the data validation library that powers FastAPI. Define models with type hints and Pydantic validates incoming JSON, generates schemas, and serializes responses automatically.
Flask-SQLAlchemy is the Flask extension for database ORM. It provides session management tied to Flask's request lifecycle but does not support async out of the box.
Starlette is the ASGI framework underneath FastAPI. It provides WebSocket support, background tasks, and middleware. You can use Starlette directly for minimal async web services.
When deploying to AWS, common patterns are ECS Fargate for containers, Elastic Beanstalk for managed deploys, or Lambda with Mangum adapter for serverless FastAPI.
Jinja2 is the template engine used by Flask and Django (with a compatible syntax). It supports inheritance, macros, and autoescaping for safe HTML rendering.
Async context managers in Python use async with syntax. FastAPI uses this for dependency lifecycle like database sessions: async with get_session() as session.
WebSocket support in FastAPI is straightforward via @app.websocket decorator. Use it for chat apps, live dashboards, and real-time notifications with bidirectional communication.
Environment variables are the standard way to configure Python apps. Use python-dotenv to load a .env file in development, and set real env vars in production.
Type hints in Python 3.10+ enable runtime validation with libraries like Pydantic. FastAPI uses type hints on function parameters to automatically parse query params, path params, and request bodies.
Rate limiting in FastAPI can be implemented with slowapi (a port of Flask-Limiter). Wrap endpoints with @limiter.limit to prevent abuse.
Caching in web apps commonly uses Redis for shared cache across instances, or functools.lru_cache for in-process caching of pure functions.
Django Channels adds WebSocket and async support to Django via an ASGI layer. It is required for Django apps that need real-time features beyond plain HTTP.
HTTPX is the async HTTP client library for Python. Use it instead of requests when building async services that need to call external APIs without blocking.
For REST API performance, key factors are: async I/O, connection pooling, response compression, and caching headers. FastAPI handles most of these patterns idiomatically.
Uvloop is a drop-in replacement for asyncio event loop built on libuv. Install uvicorn[standard] to get uvloop and httptools for 2-4x performance boost on Linux.
